title: Desk Hutch for File Cubby Base Desk
summary: A shallow open-shelf and cubby hutch that sits on top of a file-cubby desk base — three dividers split it into a shelving side and an open side.
description:
This hutch is a simple open box divided into two zones by a single off-center vertical divider: a shelved section on one side for books or bins, and a taller open section on the other for anything that doesn't fit a fixed shelf height. Building the top, sides, and divider as one assembly first — before the shelf and the smaller dividers inside the shelving zone go in — keeps the main structure easy to square up while it's still a simple open frame.

The shelving-area dividers and the plywood back are both called out as "cut to fit" rather than fixed dimensions, because small variations in how square your main assembly comes out will shift those numbers slightly. Measure your actual opening at that stage rather than trusting the plan's number to the fraction.

materials.name: 1/4" backing material (plywood, MDF, or paneling), quarter sheet

cutList.note: Updated inside width — this side is 27-1/2", not 21".
Cut to fit your assembled shelving opening.
Cut to fit the assembled hutch's actual back opening.

steps.title: Build the main frame
steps.body: Attach the top to the two outer sides and the center divider, using 2" screws, 3/4" pocket holes with 1-1/4" pocket hole screws, or 1-1/4" brad nails and glue — this joint gets reinforced by the back panel later, so any of these methods works. The open area's inside width is 27-1/2", not 21" — use the updated number.

steps.title: Attach the base supports
steps.body: Attach the shelving-area base and the open-area base (updated to 27-1/2" long) across the bottom. Nailing works, but pocket holes are worth using at the center for extra strength. No Kreg jig? Nail or screw in at an angle instead.

steps.title: Add the shelf and dividers
steps.body: Add the shelving-area bottom shelf, then the three interior dividers — same fastener choice as the base supports.

steps.title: Attach the back
steps.body: Measure the assembled hutch's actual back opening, cut the backing material to fit, and attach it with 3/4" brad nails and glue.
materials: 1/4" backing material (plywood, MDF, or paneling), quarter sheet

images.alt: Desk hutch with open shelving and a cubby section
